howItWorks.intro: This house affordability calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Front-end ratio: housing costs ≤ 28% of gross income
Back-end ratio: total debt ≤ 36% of gross income
Max loan calculated from affordable monthly P&I at given rate
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much house can I afford on my salary?
faq.answer: General rule: 2.5-3× your annual income. On $85K salary, that's $212K-255K. With a large down payment, you can go higher. The real limit is your monthly payment relative to income (28% rule).

faq.question: What is the 28/36 rule?
faq.answer: Lenders want: no more than 28% of gross income on housing (front-end DTI) and no more than 36% on all debt combined (back-end DTI). Some loan programs allow up to 43-50% back-end DTI.

faq.question: How much should I put down?
faq.answer: 20% avoids PMI ($100-300/month savings). 10% is a strong position. 3-5% is the minimum for conventional loans. FHA allows 3.5%. VA and USDA allow 0% down for eligible borrowers.

howWeCalculate.intro: This house affordability calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
Our algorithm applies the relevant formulas using your inputs
Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
Review the detailed breakdown to understand how each factor affects your result
howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This house affordability calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable estimates.

When comparing different options or scenarios side by side
When planning a budget and need to understand potential costs
